title: Restyle zoom/standard view toggle to match Filter By Distance button
description:
Two problems with the current zoom/standard view toggle button:

1. Invisible on standard view: button has white background and blends into the
   white toolbar — only visible on hover.

2. Confusing label: when in zoom view it says 'Standard View', and when in
   standard view it says 'Zoom View' (i.e. it labels the OTHER view, not the
   current state). This is the opposite of what users expect from a toggle.

Fix: restyle to match the 'Filter By Distance' toggle pattern:
  - Label is always 'Zoom View' (static, describes the mode)
  - btn-primary when zoom view is ON, btn-light when OFF
  - fa-icon toggle-on/toggle-off prefix (same as Filter By Distance)

Before (current):
  <button [ngClass]="{'btn-primary': zoomView, 'btn-outline-secondary': !zoomView}">
    {{zoomView ? 'Standard View' : 'Zoom View'}}
  </button>

After:
  <button [ngClass]="{'btn-primary': zoomView, 'btn-light': !zoomView}">
    <fa-icon *ngIf="zoomView"  icon="toggle-on"  class="mr-2"></fa-icon>
    <fa-icon *ngIf="!zoomView" icon="toggle-off" class="mr-2"></fa-icon>
    Zoom View
  </button>

File: src/app/images-tagger/images-tagger.component.html
close_reason: Fixed in GCPEditorPro commit e29a4aa

title: Review and clean up emlid2gcp.py CLI arguments
description:
Audit the CLI argument surface of GCPSighter/emlid2gcp.py and clean up anything that doesn't make sense or isn't needed.

Specific questions to resolve:
- --out-dir: reasonable but the help text still mentions 'gcpeditpro.json' which no longer exists (we removed JSON output). Update help text.
- --b1-only: debug/development flag that runs only the footprint-matching stage and prints results without writing files. Useful for development but questionable as a user-facing flag. Consider: rename to --dry-run, remove entirely, or keep with better docs.
- --nadir-only: filters to nadir images only. Reasonable but rarely used. Keep with good docs.
- --threads: keep, useful for large datasets.
- --radius: keep, footprint fallback.
- --reconstruction: keep, enables Mode B.

Action items:
1. Fix --out-dir help text (remove mention of gcpeditpro.json)
2. Decide fate of --b1-only (rename, remove, or document better)
3. Confirm all other flags are needed and help text is accurate
4. Commit to geo repo
close_reason: Fixed --out-dir help text (removed stale gcpeditpro.json mention). Renamed --b1-only to --match-only with plain-language help text.

title: GCPEditorPro: confidence column support
description:
Four sub-changes:
1. Remove sidecar JSON input field from load-config-txt UI (the optional gcpeditpro.json / gcp_estimates.json picker — no longer needed now that confidence lives in gcp_list.txt).
2. Parse 'confidence' column (column 7, 0-indexed) from gcp_list.txt in gcps-utils.service.ts. Add 'confidence?: string' field to ImageGcp. If column absent, default to 'unknown'.
3. In images-tagger.component.ts pin() method, set desc.image.confidence = 'mouse_click' when user clicks to position a GCP.
4. In export-config.component.ts (or wherever gcp_list.txt is generated for download), include the confidence field as the 8th tab-separated column. Rebuild and commit.

title: Convert auto-GCP to standalone WebODM marketplace plugin
description:
Currently the plugin lives in webodm/coreplugins/auto_gcp/ — embedded in our WebODM fork. This makes it unusable by anyone else.

Design notes and full recommendation: docs/webodm_marketplace_plugin.md

Summary of work:
1. Create a new standalone repo (e.g. webodm-plugin-auto-gcp)
2. Move plugin.py, manifest.json, api_views.py, pipeline.py, public/, requirements.txt into it
3. Update requirements.txt to list scipy (and numpy) — WebODM auto-runs pip install -r requirements.txt on plugin install
4. Add exiftool health check: plugin.py startup check via shutil.which('exiftool'); disable button + show tooltip if missing
5. Add health check API endpoint GET /api/plugins/auto_gcp/health returning JSON status of all dependencies
6. TaskView.jsx: call health endpoint on mount, show disabled button with tooltip if deps missing
7. Update manifest.json with proper metadata
8. Write README with Docker extension instructions for exiftool
9. Test install via media/plugins/ drop-in and via WebODM plugin manager
10. (Optional) Submit to WebODM plugins registry

Repo recommendation: YES separate repo. See docs/webodm_marketplace_plugin.md for rationale and pipeline sync strategy options.

Pipeline sync: keep a copy in the plugin repo for now (option 2 in the doc). Long-term: publish geo pipeline as a pip package.
notes: Naming decision (2026-03-03): Plugin name = Button Label = 'GCPSighter'.  initial version will use the emlid2gcp.py script underneath, but later versions might provide other low-level scripts.
close_reason: GCPSighter plugin created in geo/GCPSighter/ with emlid2gcp.py, all plugin files, and README. Tested successfully in WebODM via zip upload.

title: Crosshair style: use crosshair.png in sub-images, black=unconfirmed green=confirmed
description:
Two inconsistencies to fix:

1. SUB-IMAGE CANVAS USES + LINES, LARGE IMAGE USES crosshair.png
Sub-image crop component draws canvas lines (+ shape). Large image uses crosshair.png overlay.
Fix: load crosshair.png at module level (const _crosshairImg = new Image(); _crosshairImg.src = 'assets/crosshair.png').
In _draw(): draw the PNG with ctx.filter for color, centered at GCP pixel position (px-16, py-16) since crosshair is 32x32.
Filter: confirmed = 'hue-rotate(100deg) saturate(3) brightness(1.2)' (green), else 'brightness(0)' (black).

2. COLOR CONVENTION
Current: yellow=unconfirmed estimate, green=confirmed.
New: black=unconfirmed/estimated, green=confirmed (manually placed).
The yellow convention is harder to see and less intuitive than black.

Changes needed:
- smartimage.component.html: filter ternary from '...green...' : 'none' to '...green...' : 'brightness(0)'
- smartimage.component.ts: default pinColor = 'black' (was 'yellow')
- images-tagger.component.html: update [pinColor] bindings to use 'black' for unconfirmed in both standard and zoom views
- sub-image-crop.component.ts: replace canvas line drawing with crosshair.png draw + ctx.filter

Files: smartimage.component.html, smartimage.component.ts, sub-image-crop.component.ts, images-tagger.component.html
notes: crosshair.png is 32x32, lives at assets/crosshair.png. ctx.filter works in Chromium (Electron). Black=unconfirmed is more legible than yellow.
close_reason: Implemented: crosshair.png drawn in sub-image canvas. Final direction: no color filter on crosshair in any view — unmodified icon everywhere. Border colors on sub-image crops continue to indicate confirmed/estimated/untagged state.

title: GCPEditorPro: allow disassociating an image from a GCP
description:
The standard grid view already has an X button next to each image name that removes the image from the GCP's associated list (or untags the GCP from that image if the image has other GCPs). The zoom view sub-image column lacks this.

CHANGE REQUIRED:
Add an X button to each sub-image entry in the zoom view crop column, matching the behavior of the standard view X button. When clicked, it should call the same remove(desc) method already used by the standard view.

IMPLEMENTATION:
In images-tagger.component.html, inside the zoom view crop column (*ngFor loop over images), add an X/close element overlaid on or adjacent to each app-sub-image-crop entry. Style to match the standard view badge close button. Wire to (click)='remove(desc)' with $event.stopPropagation() to prevent hover-select from interfering.

Reference the standard view implementation (lines ~106-108 in images-tagger.component.html) for the button markup and behavior.
close_reason: X button added to zoom view crop column. Fixed remove() logic: wasTagged captured before unpin, otherGcps+tagged now removes from view (not just unpins), selectedDesc updated after removal.

If found: (1) inject imageGcps with estimated px/py per GCP+image, (2) pre-add image filenames to storage.images list, (3) set confirmed=false per imageGcp (for G3 integration). Requires R2 findings. See docs/gcpeditorpro_ui_spec.md for sidecar format.

title: Arrow key zoom in/out for hovered image in images-tagger
description:
In images-tagger, the user wants Up/Down arrow keys to zoom in/out the image currently under the mouse cursor. Convention: Up = zoom in (toward subject), Down = zoom out.

CONTEXT:
- SmartimageComponent uses @panzoom/panzoom library (already present)
- panzoom instance is at this.panzoom in smartimage.component.ts
- panzoom.zoomIn() / panzoom.zoomOut() advance by configured step (currently 0.7)
- Browser key-repeat fires at OS repeat rate when key is held — no custom debounce needed
- Single click to place/move GCP pin must NOT change (line 95-106 in smartimage.component.ts, 250ms debounce from panzoom events)
- Arrow key zoom is completely independent from click behavior — no conflict

IMPLEMENTATION:
In smartimage.component.ts:
1. Add a private isHovered: boolean = false flag
2. On 'mouseenter' event on the container: set isHovered = true
   (container = this.img.nativeElement.parentElement, same element mouseleave already listens on line 122)
3. On 'mouseleave': set isHovered = false (already handled, just add the flag)
4. Add @HostListener('document:keydown', ['']) onKeyDown(e: KeyboardEvent):
   - If !isHovered: return (ignore — not this instance's image)
   - If e.key === 'ArrowUp': e.preventDefault(); this.panzoom.zoomIn();
   - If e.key === 'ArrowDown': e.preventDefault(); this.panzoom.zoomOut();
   (preventDefault stops page scrolling on arrow keys)

APPLIES TO:
- Standard grid view: multiple smartimage instances visible; only hovered one responds
- Zoom view right panel: same smartimage component, same logic

DOES NOT CHANGE:
- Click behavior (single click places/moves GCP pin — central action of the app)
- Shift+wheel zoom (keep as-is)
- Any other keyboard shortcuts
notes: panzoom already has zoomIn()/zoomOut(). Just need hover tracking + keydown listener. Do not touch click/pin behavior.
close_reason: Won't fix — shift+wheel zoom in the large panel is sufficient. Arrow key zoom not needed.

title: Implement pinhole camera model for im_x,y estimation

Find all hardcoded references to 'gcp_estimates.txt' and 'gcp_estimates.json' in the WebODM plugin code (webodm/coreplugins/auto_gcp/) and update them to 'gcpeditpro.txt' and 'gcpeditpro.json'.
close_reason: Renamed in WebODM plugin (api_views.py, TaskView.jsx, TaskView.js) in commit fb1f2085.

title: Benchmark parallelism on large dataset and evaluate scaling options
description:
The pipeline currently has two parallelism contexts:

1. FOOTPRINT MATCHING (match_images_to_gcps):
   - Uses ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=threads)
   - Work is pure Python math (no I/O) — GIL limits true parallelism
   - For N images x M GCPs: O(N*M) distance checks — trivially fast even sequentially
   - WebODM path uses threads=1 (sequential) to avoid gunicorn deadlock

2. PIXEL PROJECTION (_compute_estimates_mode_a / run_pipeline Mode B):
   - Currently sequential loop — no parallelism at all
   - Mode A: pure math, trivially fast, parallelism unlikely to matter
   - Mode B: involves scipy Rotation.from_rotvec per image — more expensive, potentially worth parallelizing

EXPERIMENT GOALS:
1. Benchmark on a large dataset (100+ images, 10+ GCPs):
   - Measure wall time for each stage (EXIF read, footprint match, pixel projection, output write)
   - Identify which stage dominates
   - Measure exiftool batch call time (likely I/O-bound, but exiftool itself is fast)

2. Evaluate footprint match parallelism:
   - Test threads=1 vs threads=N for N in [2,4,8,cpu_count]
   - Is the GIL actually limiting? (footprint math is pure Python, so likely yes)
   - If GIL-limited: consider ProcessPoolExecutor for footprint match in CLI context
     (OK for CLI; still not OK inside gunicorn)

3. Evaluate pixel projection parallelism:
   - Profile Mode B per image: is scipy Rotation expensive enough to parallelize?
   - If yes: add ThreadPoolExecutor to _compute_estimates_mode_b() in CLI context

4. Evaluate exiftool parallelism:
   - Currently one batch call for all images (already optimal — single exiftool invocation)
   - No further optimization needed

5. Recommend whether any parallelism improvements are worth implementing, and at
   what dataset scale they become meaningful.

CONTEXT:
- WebODM always uses threads=1 (sequential) — do not change this
- CLI uses threads=0 (all CPUs) by default — this is where parallelism matters
- GIL note: ThreadPoolExecutor is fork-safe but GIL-limited for pure Python CPU work;
  ProcessPoolExecutor gives true parallelism but risks deadlock if used inside gunicorn

Output: update this bead with findings and create follow-up beads if improvements are warranted.
notes: Stage timing analysis first. Footprint match is O(N*M) trivial math; projection is where Mode B cost lives. CLI vs WebODM context matters.

title: Draw sub-image crop bounding box on large image in zoom view
description:
In the zoom view's large image panel, draw a colored bounding box showing the
region that the currently-selected sub-image crop covers.

## What to draw
The sub-image crop is a 256×256 px window centered on the GCP pixel (imX, imY),
clamped to image bounds (same logic as SubImageCropComponent._draw):
  sx = max(0, min(imX - 128, imgW - 256))
  sy = max(0, min(imY - 128, imgH - 256))
  rect = (sx, sy, 256, 256)

## Color
Matches the sub-image border color (same as SubImageCropComponent.borderColor):
  confirmed → green  (#28a745)
  estimated → yellow (#ffc107)
If isTagged is false (manually added image, not yet clicked), skip the box.

## Implementation approach
1. Add @Input() cropBox: {x,y,w,h,color} | null to SmartimageComponent.
2. Inside the panzoom container div (parent of the img), add a positioned overlay
   div using left/top/width/height in image-pixel space — it shares the panzoom
   transform so it scales/pans correctly with the image.
3. In images-tagger.component.html, bind cropBox on the zoom-view app-smartimage:
   [cropBox]="selectedDesc ? getCropBox(selectedDesc) : null"
4. Add getCropBox(desc) in images-tagger.component.ts: reads imX/imY, gets image
   natural dimensions from the SmartimageComponent @ViewChild, computes sx/sy.

## Behaviour
- Box updates whenever selectedDesc changes (hover → new sub-image selected).
- Click to reposition GCP still works unchanged; box moves to new position.
close_reason: Implemented and committed (34e296d): syncCropBox() in SmartimageComponent draws 256×256 bounding box on large image matching sub-image crop color (green=confirmed, yellow=estimated). Tracks pan/zoom.

title: Research physical GCP target types
description: Research other gcp target types, including prices, time cost (setup/pickup), and time savings (auto-detection). Link: https://community.opendronemap.org/t/favorite-physical-media-for-ground-control-points-gcps/23304. Note ArUco tags or apriltags options.
close_reason: Research complete: ArUco/AprilTag 24x24 Coroplast signs recommended at 300ft flight altitude, findings documented in issue comments

comments.author: Jon Stearley
comments.text:
GCP Sizing Researcher Findings (Flight Alt: 300'):
- Calculated GSD: 3.38 cm/pixel (based on DJI Focal Length: 12.29mm).
- ArUco/AprilTag Target: 24" x 24" (~60cm) required for reliable auto-detection (3 pixels per bit).
- Checkerboard Target: 14" (~35cm) is sufficient for manual tagging.
- Cost (Set of 10): ~- for DIY 24" Coroplast signs vs + for commercial rigid targets.
- Recommendation: 24" Coroplast signs with ArUco ID patterns for automated ID mapping.

title: Verify GCPEditorPro confirmed-GCP download matches pipeline; unify format
description:
## Goal
Confirm that the file downloaded from GCPEditorPro after a user confirms GCPs
matches what gcp_pipeline.py produces (same coordinates, same images), and make
the two formats identical so a plain `diff` can be used for comparison.

## Current format mismatch (observed 2026-02-26)

### gcpeditpro.txt (pipeline, _write_gcp_list):
  - Space-separated columns
  - 4 decimal places with trailing zeros: 1666970.6970  1596792.0180  6355.1720
  - Pixel coords also padded: 1813.30  536.20

### gcp_list.txt (GCPEditorPro download):
  - Tab-separated columns
  - Trailing zeros stripped: 1666970.697  1596792.018  6355.172  1813.3  536.2

## Fix (gcp_pipeline.py _write_gcp_list)
1. Change separator from spaces to tabs (match GCPEditorPro)
2. Strip trailing zeros from geo coords and pixel coords, e.g.:
     f"{v:.3f}".rstrip('0').rstrip('.')   # geo coords (mm precision)
     f"{v:.2f}".rstrip('0').rstrip('.')   # pixel coords

## Verification workflow
After fixing the format:
  1. Run pipeline on a real dataset → gcpeditpro.txt
  2. Load gcpeditpro.txt into GCPEditorPro, confirm a few GCPs, download gcp_list.txt
  3. Sort both files (excluding header) and diff — should show only the confirmed/corrected rows
     (the actual px/py will differ for confirmed GCPs; unconfirmed rows should be identical)
close_reason: Format unified (tabs, trailing-zero stripping) in commit cc36175. Verified with diff after GCPEditorPro confirm: exactly one line differs (the clicked image), all others identical. Bonus: discovered and fixed two bugs in images-tagger (load all images OOM, save from filtered list causes data loss) in GCPEditorPro commit 6bd8615.

title: Create training data for GCP points

title: Research alternative GCP tagging tools
close_reason: Orange clay pigeon path chosen; alternative tagging tools research deprioritized. See geo-2j8 epic.

comments.author: Jon Stearley
comments.text:
Research summary:
- YOLO (v5/v8): Faster, requires custom training for markers.
- ResNet/RetinaNet: Higher precision (sub-pixel), high compute.
- Find-GCP: Classical CV approach using ArUco markers.
- GCPEditorPro: Uses OpenCV/Checkerboard detection.
Conclusion: Professional tools like Metashape/Pix4D use a hybrid 'Pinhole + Refinement' approach. Standalone open-source versions for general markers are rare.

title: Implement pixel projection for image+GCP pairs (EXIF and reconstruction modes)
description: In geo/gcp_pipeline.py. Mode A (EXIF): use GimbalPitchDegree/Yaw/Roll + GPS + altitude per docs/dji_m3e_camera_model.md — derive sensor dims from FOV+focal, apply ENU rotation, pinhole project. Mode B (reconstruction.json): load SfM-refined camera poses from opensfm/reconstruction.json. Auto-detect: use Mode B if reconstruction_path provided, else Mode A. One worker per (image, GCP) pair via multiprocessing.Pool. Returns {(image_path, gcp_label): (px, py)}.
close_reason: B2 implemented in gcp_pipeline.py: project_pixel_mode_a() (validated 73.6px mean error) and project_pixel_mode_b() (reconstruction.json, pending validation when ODM output available).

title: Rename gcp.py to copy_gcp_images.py and clean up CLI
description:
gcp.py is solely responsible for copying relevant drone images (those containing listed GCP points) from a full image set into a smaller output directory. Rename and clean up accordingly.

Work:
1. Rename gcp.py to copy_gcp_images.py
2. Review and clean up CLI arguments:
   - gcp_csv + image_dir: keep (core inputs)
   - --radius: keep
   - --closest-three: keep (useful subset selection)
   - --select-points: keep (explicit label filter)
   - --no-output-dir: odd flag — if the only purpose is copying images, what does 'no output dir' mean? Investigate and either remove or rename to something clearer
   - --threads: keep
3. Update script description/docstring to reflect actual purpose
4. Update any references in README or other docs
5. Commit to geo repo
close_reason: Renamed gcp.py to copy_gcp_images.py. Updated docstring, argparse description, --no-output-dir renamed to --list-only.

title: Implement confirmation state tracking with sidecar .confirmed.json
description: In ~/git/GCPEditorPro/src/app/. (1) Add confirmed:boolean to ImageGcp type in gcps-utils.service.ts, (2) shift-click in images-tagger sets confirmed=true + updates pin color yellow→green, (3) Write/read .confirmed.json sidecar alongside .txt file: {gcpLabel:{filename:true}}. See docs/gcpeditorpro_ui_spec.md.

title: Zoom view: unified click-to-pin + hover-to-preview
description:
All image interaction behaviors should be identical between sub-image crops (left column) and the large panel (right):
- drag-to-pan
- click-to-position-GCP crosshair
- shift+mouse-wheel to zoom

The ONLY difference: hovering over a sub-image loads that image into the large panel at right.

IMPLEMENTATION NOTES:
Currently the sub-image crop is a fixed 256x256 canvas with no panzoom. To support drag-to-pan and shift+wheel zoom, sub-images need panzoom too (or be replaced by app-smartimage instances). The large panel already uses app-smartimage with full panzoom support.

Options to consider:
1. Replace sub-image canvas with app-smartimage instances in a fixed 256x256 container (autoFit=true, small size) — reuses existing component, gets pan/zoom/click for free.
2. Add panzoom to the canvas in SubImageCropComponent — more work, keeps canvas drawing.

Option 1 is likely simpler. The crop centering (showing the area around the estimated GCP) would need to be handled by setting the initial pan/zoom to center on pinLocation, which is related to geo-mz0 (auto-zoom to GCP on load).

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- Drag pans the image in both sub and large windows
- Shift+wheel zooms in both sub and large windows
- Click positions the GCP crosshair in both sub and large windows
- Hovering a sub-image loads it into the large panel (existing behavior, keep)
close_reason: Won't fix — current behavior (hover-to-load into large panel, click-to-pin in both views) is sufficient. Full panzoom parity (drag-to-pan, shift+wheel) in sub-image crops not pursued.

title: Add non-nadir image detection and warning in Mode A projection
description:
gcp_pipeline.py Mode A (project_pixel_mode_a) assumes nadir imagery (camera pointing straight down, pitch ≈ -90°). This is baked into the math — the gimbal_pitch EXIF field is read but never used in the projection.

If given oblique images (e.g. from a handheld camera, or a drone in non-nadir mission mode), Mode A will silently produce wrong pixel estimates with no warning.

Work to do:
1. In project_pixel_mode_a(): check gimbal_pitch if available.
   If abs(gimbal_pitch - (-90.0)) > NADIR_TOLERANCE_DEG (suggest 15°), skip the image
   and return None with a logged warning, OR add a 'nadir_warn' flag to the return.

2. In _footprint_worker() / match_images_to_gcps(): if pitch is non-nadir,
   either skip the image or use a conservative fallback radius (footprint shape is
   wrong for oblique).

3. In run_pipeline(): collect and summarize non-nadir images in a warning message
   at the end (e.g. '3 images skipped: non-nadir pitch (Mode A only handles nadir)').

4. Mode B (project_pixel_mode_b) is unaffected — it uses the full rotation matrix
   from reconstruction.json and handles any orientation.

5. Add a --allow-oblique flag to the CLI to suppress the skip behavior if user
   explicitly wants to try oblique images anyway.

Design note: NADIR_TOLERANCE_DEG = 15 means any pitch outside [-105°, -75°] is
flagged. DJI drones in nadir mode typically report -90°.
notes: gimbal_pitch is read in EXIF batch but never used in projection math. Oblique images produce silently wrong results.
close_reason: Implemented full 3D rotation matrix (pitch+yaw) in project_pixel_mode_a() — handles both nadir and oblique correctly. Nadir case is mathematically identical to old formula. Added --nadir-only flag to skip oblique images when desired. Commits a60f630 and c0804a7.

title: Auto-zoom images to GCP estimate location on load
description:
When an image loads in images-tagger and a GCP pixel estimate exists (pinLocation is set), the view should automatically pan+zoom to show the area around that GCP point. Currently images load showing the full image at 1:1 scale — GCP targets are typically tiny relative to the full image, requiring manual zoom and pan every time.

DESIRED UX:
- Image loads → immediately zoomed in and centered on the estimated GCP pixel
- Default zoom level = show ~10 sq meters of ground area (user-configurable setting)
- A '10 sq meter view' at 3cm GSD (typical drone survey) = ~333x333 pixels shown → tight enough to see the GCP target clearly

IMPLEMENTATION PLAN:

1. PIPELINE CHANGE (gcp_pipeline.py / gcp_estimates.json):
   Add GSD (ground sample distance, cm/pixel) per image to estimates JSON.
   GSD can be computed from EXIF data already available in the pipeline:
     focal_mm, focal35_mm, img_w, img_h, agl (abs_alt - site_ellip_alt_m)
     GSD_cm = (sensor_width_mm / focal_mm) * agl_m * 100 / img_w
   Include in estimates JSON per image:
     { 'px': ..., 'py': ..., 'mode': ..., 'gsd_cm': 3.2 }

2. GCPEDITORPRO CHANGE (smartimage.component.ts):
   Add @Input() autoZoomSqM: number = 10 (configurable ground area in sq meters)
   Add @Input() gsdCm: number | null = null (from estimates sidecar)
   
   After image loads (img load event) and pinLocation is set:
   a. If gsdCm is available: compute target pixel extent
        side_px = sqrt(autoZoomSqM * 10000) / gsdCm   [10000 cm² per m²]
        scale = img_w / side_px  (approximate zoom level)
   b. Else (no GSD): fall back to showing ~400px around GCP (reasonable default)
   c. Call panzoom.zoom(scale) then pan to center pinLocation in the viewport
      panzoom has zoomToPoint(scale, point) which handles both in one call

3. SETTINGS:
   Add a configurable 'autoZoomSqM' setting (localStorage persisted) with default 10.
   Display in images-tagger toolbar or a settings panel.
   Set to 0 or null to disable auto-zoom (show full image).

NOTES:
- This applies to both Standard Grid View and Zoom View right panel
- If no pinLocation (no estimate): do not auto-zoom (show full image)
- panzoom.zoomToPoint(scale, focalPoint) is the key API call
  where focalPoint is in container-relative pixel coordinates
- The 250ms panzoom event debounce for pin placement is unrelated and unchanged
- GSD fallback (no EXIF data): use 400px default extent

PIPELINE SYNC:
After changing gcp_pipeline.py, sync to webodm/coreplugins/auto_gcp/pipeline.py
notes: GSD is already computable in the pipeline from EXIF (focal, sensor, agl). Add to estimates JSON. SmartimageComponent reads it and calls panzoom.zoomToPoint() on image load. Default 10 sq meters.
close_reason: Auto-zoom on load satisfied by _fitToContainer() which fits the image to the panel when autoFit=true. GSD-based zoom level and associated pipeline/JSON/UI changes dropped as unnecessary complexity.

title: Implement zoomed sub-image column view in images-tagger
description: In images-tagger.component.ts/.html. New layout mode: left column of 256x256 crops centered at estimated GCP pixel (Canvas API, load full image once then draw crops), right panel = full image of selected crop with pan+zoom+shift-click. Toggle button Zoom View / Standard View, persisted in localStorage. Sub-image outline: gray=unseen, yellow=estimated, green=confirmed. Default selection: first unconfirmed sub-image. See docs/gcpeditorpro_ui_spec.md.
close_reason: Implemented: SubImageCropComponent (Canvas 256x256 crops, border colors), Zoom View two-panel layout, image folder picker on load-config-txt, confirmed=true on pin. Build clean, user tested.

title: explore lightning
description:
https://webodm.net/pricing shows reasonable pricing.  need to review the sizes of typical projects - i suspect GB is the limiter, not number of maps.  and how are maps counted?  probably each rerun is counted, is ortho 1, and tin 1, and etc - such that a single project could have multiple "maps" resulting from a single run?  i'm thinking the Pro level would be sufficient.  but how many projects until the 100G limit is hit?  thus the need for webodm s3 plugin (bead geo-sut).
also should compare costs with an approach of running webodm on ec2 node, with s3 "mounted" (there's probably a way to do that?), then just add project and images in webodm in the usual way (selecting from the s3 "disk") - instead of writing the mentioned s3 plugin.  i'm guessing lighting is the best deal, modulo the storage, which could be mitigated by the s3 plugin...

title: Zoom view UX fixes: fit-to-panel, hover-to-select, click=set GCP
description:
Follow-up to geo-n2j. Three related zoom view issues found during first use.

1. FIT TO PANEL
Right panel smartimage shows upper-left corner (natural pixel size overflows panel). Needs zoom-to-fit on load.
- Add autoFit: boolean = false input to SmartimageComponent
- Persistent img load event listener (handles src changes + cached images): if autoFit, call _fitToContainer()
- _fitToContainer(): scale = min(cw/nw, ch/nh); panzoom.zoom(scale, {animate:false}); panzoom.pan((cw-nw*scale)/2, (ch-nh*scale)/2, {animate:false})
- Pass [autoFit]='true' to right-panel smartimage in images-tagger zoom view only

2. HOVER-TO-SELECT (replaces click-to-select)
Click in sub-image should set GCP, not select for right panel.
Fix: add (mouseenter)='selectDesc(desc)' on each crop wrapper div in images-tagger.html.

3. CLICK IN SUB-IMAGE = SET GCP
Rename selectCrop output to clickPosition, emit {x,y} in full-image coords.
In sub-image-crop onCanvasClick(e): sx = max(0, min(imX-128, naturalWidth-256)); emit { x: sx+e.offsetX, y: sy+e.offsetY }.
In images-tagger: add pinFromSubImage(pos, desc) = selectDesc(desc) + pin(pos, desc).

ISSUE: Crosshair position inconsistency between sub-image and large panel is likely caused by pin math (getPinLocation) breaking when image overflows container — fixed by fix 1.
notes: Issues 1 and crosshair inconsistency are coupled. autoFit must be zoom-view-only (standard grid already constrains by overflow:hidden).
close_reason: Implemented: autoFit input + _fitToContainer (explicit CSS px size so panzoom scale=1=fit), hover-to-select via mouseenter, click-in-sub-image sets GCP via pinFromSubImage. GCP-anchored zoom via zoomToPoint. All committed.

title: Show confirmed/total count badge on GCP list page
description: In gcps-map.component.ts/.html. For each GCP show badge showing c/t where c=confirmed images, t=total images in estimates. Color: red=0 confirmed, yellow=partial, green=all confirmed. Reads from storage.imageGcps confirmed field (requires G3). See docs/gcpeditorpro_ui_spec.md.

title: Update setup_env.sh with all pipeline dependencies
description:
setup_env.sh currently installs: gdal, flask, tk, pyproj, ezdxf, python=3.10 via conda.

Missing for gcp_pipeline.py:
- scipy (required for Mode B projection via scipy.spatial.transform.Rotation)
- numpy (required for Mode B, likely pulled in by scipy but should be explicit)

System dependency (cannot be conda-installed):
- exiftool — called via subprocess by read_image_exif_batch(). Install with:
  - macOS: brew install exiftool
  - Ubuntu/Debian: apt-get install libimage-exiftool-perl

Audit needed:
- flask, tk: likely legacy from old gcp.py GUI code or app.py — verify still needed
- ezdxf: needed for contour DXF output in gcp.py — verify still needed

Tasks:
1. Audit all scripts in geo/ repo for their actual imports
2. Add scipy, numpy to conda create command
3. Add a comment block documenting exiftool as a system dep with install instructions
4. Remove any packages that are no longer needed (after audit)
5. Consider adding a verification step at end of script: python -c 'import pyproj, scipy, numpy'
6. Consider adding exiftool check: command -v exiftool || echo 'WARNING: exiftool not found'

File: /Users/jrstear/git/geo/setup_env.sh
notes: gcp_pipeline.py Mode B requires scipy+numpy. exiftool is a system dep that cannot go in conda.

Research exact Pix4D GCP image file format before implementing (column names, delimiter, header line, coordinate order). File should be named pix4d.txt and placed next to the main output file.

title: Add server-side auto-detect to WebODM plugin and results UI in GCPEditorPro
description: Two parts: (1) WebODM plugin adds /detect endpoint: accepts task_id + classifier_type (haar|cnn|yolo) + crop_size, loads confirmed sub-images from .confirmed.json as positives, runs inference server-side (Python, can use GPU), returns detected pixels per (image,GCP). Heavy compute server-side — Haar remains available in-browser via OpenCV.js as fallback. (2) GCPEditorPro: Detect GCPs button calls WebODM detect endpoint, shows results as yellow estimated pins. Classifier selection dropdown + crop bounds selector.

title: Remove pagination nav from zoom view in images-tagger
description:
In images-tagger, the bottom pagination bar (ngb-pagination) is always rendered
regardless of view mode. In zoom view it is useless — all images are visible in
the scrollable left column, there is no paging — and clicking it does nothing
visible.

Fix: wrap the ngb-pagination element in *ngIf="!zoomView" so it only appears
in standard view. The 'Cancel' and 'Save Changes' buttons should remain visible
in both views.

File: src/app/images-tagger/images-tagger.component.html
The pagination is on the fixed-bottom bar, roughly:
  <ngb-pagination *ngIf="images.length > pageSize" ...>
Add the !zoomView condition:
  <ngb-pagination *ngIf="!zoomView && images.length > pageSize" ...>
close_reason: Fixed in GCPEditorPro commit e29a4aa

title: Add +/- zoom buttons in full image view
description: In images-tagger full-image panel (G2). Add [+] and [-] buttons bottom-right (absolute positioned, styled like Google Maps zoom controls). Each click zooms 1.5x centered on current view center. Supplements scroll-wheel zoom which is too touchy at high resolution. See docs/gcpeditorpro_ui_spec.md.
close_reason: Won't fix — shift+wheel zoom in the large panel is sufficient. Dedicated +/- buttons not needed.
